# @byted-service/consul 服务发现

[![bnpm](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/badge/v/@byted-service/consul.svg?style=flat-square)](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/package/@byted-service/consul) [![download](https://2y8ngapf.fn.bytedance.net/d?p=@byted-service/consul)](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/package/@byted-service/consul)

[简体中文](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/consul/README.md) | [English](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/consul/README.en.md) | [CHANGELOG](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/consul/CHANGELOG.md)

> The following is **@byted-service/consul@2.x** document, **1.x** document see [consul-v1.x](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/tree/consul-v1.x/packages/consul), [@byted-service/consul@2.0.0 release log](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/wiki/wikcnwU1HWzEOsAiWDdkrMCdNqh).

**@byted-service/consul** is the basic service discovery `SDK` on the `Node.js` side of the company. It is widely integrated in `@byted-service/xxx`. See [Node.js Service Discovery ( Consul ) User Guide](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/wiki/wikcnU8nNhyPfhV2OjHLCVQPX5g), [A Quick Start with Service Discovery ( Consul ) for Front-End Engineers](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/wiki/wikcn9QfY7pMwo9I9Jf1Y2q6R4d).

## Installation

```bash
npm install @byted-service/consul --registry=http://bnpm.byted.org
```

## Quick Start

```ts
// consul.ts
import { lookup, lookupName, random, Consul } from '@byted-service/consul';

(async () => {
    console.log(await lookup('p.s.m'));
    console.log(await lookupName('p.s.m'));
    console.log(await random('p.s.m'));

    const consul = new Consul();

    console.log(await consul.lookup('p.s.m'));
})();
```

```ts
ROUTE_IP=10.225.130.44 ts-node consul.ts
```

PS: `10.225.130.44` is the `IP` of the public development machine.

## API

### lookup(psm[, options[, consul]])

Get the service registration address of `PSM`.

-   `psm`: Service `PSM`.
-   `options`
    -   `cluster`: Service `Cluster`.
    -   `idc`: Service `IDC`.
    -   `addrfam`: Service `address family` filter.
        -   `dual-stack`: Return `IPv4 + IPv6` address.
        -   `v4`: Return `IPv4` address.
        -   `v6`: Return `IPv6` address.
    -   `unique`: Service address merging conditions will only take effect when `options.addrfam` is `dual-stack`.
        -   `v4`: Based on `IPv4`.
        -   `v6`: Based on `IPv6`.
    -   `limit`: Return number.
    -   `nocache`: Whether not to use cache.
    -   `consulHash`: The value of `x-consul-result-hash` used when requesting `Consul Agent`.
    -   `timeout`: Request timeout, the unit is `ms`, the default value is `3s`.
-   `consul`: `Consul` instance, the default value is `new Consul()`.

### lookupName(psm[, options[, consul]])

Get the service registration address of `PSM`, which is different from `lookup()`. It will automatically add `options.addrfam` and `options.unique` parameters according to the `BYTED_HOST_IP` and `BYTED_HOST_IPV6` environment variables. See:

-   [Node.js dual-stack ( ipv4 + ipv6 ) upgrade](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/wiki/wikcnwT1YeXEEGN3zZVlzxxazEd).
-   [Infrastructure IPv6 Upgrade Manual ( For Business )](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/wiki/wikcn7ufZcNgRKH8w53IPSME4vg).
-   [Enable ipv6 support](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/wiki/wikcnVVJN7FXM6XZ6POqYMsFn1c).

<!--  -->

-   `psm`: Service `PSM`.
-   `options`
    -   `cluster`: Service `Cluster`.
    -   `idc`: Service `IDC`.
    -   `addrfam`: Service `address family` filter.
        -   `dual-stack`: Return `IPv4 + IPv6` address.
        -   `v4`: Return `IPv4` address.
        -   `v6`: Return `IPv6` address.
    -   `unique`: Service address merging conditions will only take effect when `options.addrfam` is `dual-stack`.
        -   `v4`: Based on `IPv4`.
        -   `v6`: Based on `IPv6`.
    -   `limit`: Return number.
    -   `nocache`: Whether not to use cache.
    -   `consulHash`: The value of `x-consul-result-hash` used when requesting `Consul Agent`.
    -   `timeout`: Request timeout, the unit is `ms`, the default value is `3s`.
-   `consul`: `Consul` instance, the default value is `new Consul()`.

### random(psm[, options[, consul]])

Randomly get a service registration address of `PSM` ( weighted random ).

-   `psm`: Service `PSM`.
-   `options`
    -   `cluster`: Service `Cluster`.
    -   `idc`: Service `IDC`.
    -   `addrfam`: Service `address family` filter.
        -   `dual-stack`: Return `IPv4 + IPv6` address.
        -   `v4`: Return `IPv4` address.
        -   `v6`: Return `IPv6` address.
    -   `unique`: Service address merging conditions will only take effect when `options.addrfam` is `dual-stack`.
        -   `v4`: Based on `IPv4`.
        -   `v6`: Based on `IPv6`.
    -   `limit`: Return number.
    -   `nocache`: Whether not to use cache.
    -   `consulHash`: The value of `x-consul-result-hash` used when requesting `Consul Agent`.
    -   `timeout`: Request timeout, the unit is `ms`, the default value is `3s`.
-   `consul`: `Consul` instance, the default value is `new Consul()`.

### new Consul(\[options\])

-   `options`
    -   `host`: `Consul Agent Host`.
    -   `port`: `Consul Agent Port`.
    -   `socketPath`: `Consul Agent UDS ( Unix Domain Socket )`.
    -   `logger`: `Logger` instance.
    -   `enableWarning`: Whether to output `warning`, the local default is `false`.
    -   `cacheTime`: Cache time, the unit is `ms`, the default value is `15s`.
    -   `debug`: Whether to enable `debug` ( for debugging ), the default value is `false`.

### consul.address

`Consul Agent Address`.

### consul.lookup(psm[, options])

Get the service registration address of `PSM`.

-   `psm`: Service `PSM`.
-   `options`
    -   `cluster`: Service `Cluster`.
    -   `idc`: Service `IDC`.
    -   `addrfam`: Service `address family` filter.
        -   `dual-stack`: Return `IPv4 + IPv6` address.
        -   `v4`: Return `IPv4` address.
        -   `v6`: Return `IPv6` address.
    -   `unique`: Service address merging conditions will only take effect when `options.addrfam` is `dual-stack`.
        -   `v4`: Based on `IPv4`.
        -   `v6`: Based on `IPv6`.
    -   `limit`: Return number.
    -   `nocache`: Whether not to use cache.
    -   `consulHash`: The value of `x-consul-result-hash` used when requesting `Consul Agent`.
    -   `timeout`: Request timeout, the unit is `ms`, the default value is `3s`.
-   `consul`: `Consul` instance, the default value is `new Consul()`.

## Environment Variables

### Consul Agent Uds Path

Environment variables:

-   `BYTED_SD_UDS_PATH`

```shell
# At this time, the service registration address of PSM will be got from /path/to/consul.sock.
BYTED_SD_UDS_PATH=/path/to/consul.sock node app.js
```

Priority:

-   `BYTED_SD_UDS_PATH` > Consul Agent Host

### Consul Agent Host

Environment variables:

-   `CONSUL_HTTP_HOST`
-   `MY_HOST_IP`
-   `TCE_HOST_IP`
-   `MY_HOST_IPV6`
-   `ROUTE_IP`

```shell
# At this time, the service registration address of PSM will be got from 10.225.130.44:2280.
CONSUL_HTTP_HOST=10.225.130.44 node app.js
```

Priority:

-   `CONSUL_HTTP_HOST` > `MY_HOST_IP` > `TCE_HOST_IP` > `MY_HOST_IPV6` > `ROUTE_IP`

### Consul Agent Port

Environment variables:

-   `CONSUL_HTTP_PORT`

```shell
# At this time, the service registration address of PSM will be got from 127.0.0.1:3000.
CONSUL_HTTP_HOST=127.0.0.1 CONSUL_HTTP_PORT=3000 node app.js
```

## FAQ

### ETIMEOUT & ECONNREFUSED

When developing locally, the following errors may be encountered:

```sh
Error: connect ETIMEOUT 10.17.91.134:2280
```

This is because `@byted-service/consul` will use the address `10.17.91.134:2280` for service discovery. If the address has expired or the network is isolated, the above error may occur. At this time, you can avoid this problem by setting the `CONSUL_HTTP_HOST` environment variable.

```sh
# At this time, the service registration address of PSM will be got from 10.225.130.44:2280.
CONSUL_HTTP_HOST=10.225.130.44 node app.js
```

PS: `10.225.130.44` is the `IP` of the public development machine.

### Local To Boe

There is generally no network isolation between local and `boe` ( except `boe-i18n` ), so service discovery can be performed directly.

1. Get the `Devbox` or `Boe` machine `IP` ( assuming it is `10.225.130.44` ).
2. Set the `CONSUL_HTTP_HOST` environment variable.

```sh
# At this time, the service registration address of PSM will be got from 10.225.130.44:2280.
CONSUL_HTTP_HOST=10.225.130.44 node app.js
```

PS: `TCE` machine `IP` maybe change, therefore, please use `Devbox` machine `IP` in most cases.

### Local To PPE / Prod

There is network isolation between the local and `PPE` / `Prod`, so service discovery cannot be performed directly, and you need to apply for escape first, see [Office-Production Network Access Restriction](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/docs/doccnkCENdx6SWyeKo3belNpj6f).

1. Get the `PPE` or `Prod` machine `IP` ( assuming it is `10.0.0.1` ).
2. Set the `CONSUL_HTTP_HOST` environment variable.

```sh
# At this time, the service registration address of PSM will be got from 10.0.0.1:2280.
CONSUL_HTTP_HOST=10.0.0.1 node app.js
```

### Boe To PPE / Prod

There is network isolation between the `Boe` and `PPE` / `Prod`, so service discovery cannot be performed directly, and you need to apply for escape first, see [BOE - Production Access Isolation](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/docs/doccnJlbtFF8bonFtJusyBysFUt).

1. Get the `PPE` or `Prod` machine `IP` ( assuming it is `10.0.0.1` ).
2. Set the `CONSUL_HTTP_HOST` environment variable.

```sh
# At this time, the service registration address of PSM will be got from 10.0.0.1:2280.
CONSUL_HTTP_HOST=10.0.0.1 node app.js
```

### Devbox to Boe

There is generally no network isolation between `Devbox` and `boe` ( except `boe-i18n` ), so service discovery can be performed directly.

1. Get the `Devbox` or `Boe` machine `IP` ( assuming it is `10.225.130.44` ).
2. Set the `CONSUL_HTTP_HOST` environment variable.

```sh
# At this time, the service registration address of PSM will be got from 10.225.130.44:2280.
CONSUL_HTTP_HOST=10.225.130.44 node app.js
```

### Devbox to PPE / Prod

There is network isolation between the `Devbox` and `PPE` / `Prod`, so service discovery cannot be performed directly, and you need to apply for escape first, see [Devbox - Production Access Isolation](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/docs/doccnI1e3EbOm9dyPiupGtUM2Lb).

1. Get the `PPE` or `Prod` machine `IP` ( assuming it is `10.0.0.1` ).
2. Set the `CONSUL_HTTP_HOST` environment variable.

```sh
# At this time, the service registration address of PSM will be got from 10.0.0.1:2280.
CONSUL_HTTP_HOST=10.0.0.1 node app.js
```
